---
applyTo: "**/src/**"
---

# Project General Coding Standards

- Consult the [backend tech-stack](/specs/technical-specs/backend-stack.md) document to understand the technologies used in this project before writing and suggesting new specs to maintain consistency.
- Consult the [frontend tech-stack](/specs/technical-specs/frontend-stack.md) document to understand the technologies used in this project before writing and suggesting new specs to maintain consistency.
- Consult the [data-models](/specs/data-models) document to understand the data structures and relationships before writing and suggesting new specs to maintain consistency.

## Code Style Guidelines

- Use english (CA) for all variables, functions, classes, etc...
- Use english (CA) for all comments and documentation.
- Follow consistent indentation (4 spaces).
- Use meaningful variable and function names.
- Write modular and reusable code.
- Include comments to explain complex logic.
- Use consistent naming conventions.
  - Use `camelCase` for variables and functions.
  - Use `PascalCase` for classes and components.
  - Use `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants.
  - Use `kebab-case` for file and folder names.
- Use double quotes for strings in JavaScript/TypeScript.
- Use semicolons at the end of statements in JavaScript/TypeScript.
- Use S.O.L.I.D principles for object-oriented design:
  - Use single responsibility principle for functions and classes.
  - Use open-closed principle for class design.
  - Use liskov substitution principle for inheritance.
  - Use interface segregation principle for defining interfaces.
  - Use dependency inversion principle for managing dependencies.
